title: Kaitiakitanga o Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku | Guardianship of the Mangakōtukutuku Stream
subject: Te Reo Māori
culturalContext: Students will explore kaitiakitanga through the lens of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku, a significant stream in our rohe that holds cultural, historical, and ecological importance to Ngāti Kahungunu. The stream's name references the native New Zealand falcon (kārearea) that once nested along its banks, and its waters supported traditional fishing practices and provided sustenance for local hapū. Students will connect with the pūrākau (traditional story) of the stream and understand their role as modern kaitiaki.
learningObjectives: Identify and use at least 10 te reo Māori kupu related to freshwater ecosystems (awa, tuna, inanga, etc.) in spoken and written contexts.
Explain the concept of kaitiakitanga in relation to Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku by creating a short pepeha for the awa.
Collaborate in a wānanga group to analyze a current environmental issue affecting the stream (e.g., water quality, invasive species) and propose a solution using Māori perspectives.
Design and present a simple rautaki (strategy) in te reo Māori for a class-led planting day to enhance the mauri of the awa.

activities.title: He Pepeha mō Te Awa | A Pepeha for the Stream
activities.description: Students will learn the pūrākau of Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku. Using this knowledge, they will collaboratively craft a pepeha (tribal proverb) for the awa, introducing it as if it were an ancestor. This will incorporate key kupu for geographical features and native species. The pepeha will be recited as a karakia to open subsequent lessons.

activities.title: He Aha Ōku Kupu? | What Are My Words?
activities.description: A hands-on 'Kupu Kete' (word basket) activity. Using flashcards, images, and realia (e.g., a water testing kit, a picture of a tuna), students will work in pairs to learn, practice, and teach each other the target vocabulary for the lesson. This will be reinforced through a competitive 'Kupu Kimi' (word hunt) game around the classroom.

activities.title: Te Tirohanga o te Kaitiaki | The Guardian's Perspective
activities.description: A wānanga (learning circle) session. The class will be divided into small groups, each assigned a different perspective: a kaumātua, a local farmer, a scientist, and a tamariki. Using prompt cards with simple te reo Māori questions (e.g., 'He aha te mate o te awa?', 'Me pēhea te whakaora?'), each group will discuss a provided water quality report and formulate a response from their assigned viewpoint.

activities.title: Te Mahi Taiao | The Environmental Action
activities.description: A practical, project-based activity. The class will develop a rautaki (strategy) for a riparian planting project along a section of the stream. This will involve creating simple instructions in te reo Māori for planting native harakeke (flax) and tī kōuka (cabbage trees), which help stabilize the banks and filter water. This plan will be the basis for their assessment.

resources: Ngā Kete Kōrero: Pūrākau o Ngāti Kahungunu (A collection of stories from the iwi)
Local Expert: Whaea Aniwa Black (Kaumātua and environmental advocate for Te Awa o Mangakōtukutuku)
Digital Tool: NIWA's 'Wai Māori' educational website and interactive river health modules
Physical Resource: 'Te Ara Takutaku' planting guides from Hawke's Bay Regional Council

tasks: Create a visual 'Rautaki Whakatō' (Planting Strategy) poster. The poster must include 5 simple steps for planting, each step described using at least one key te reo Māori kupu (e.g., 'Keri te poka' - Dig the hole).
Present your poster to a small group, verbally explaining your rautaki using the te reo Māori phrases you have written.

nzcAlignment: Te Reo Māori: Communicate about responsibilities, plans, and intentions (L5 AO 3.2); Use cultural conventions appropriately (L5 AO 3.3)
Tikanga-ā-Iwi: Understand how people manage resources (L5 AO 3.1); Understand how policies and practices reflect cultural diversity (L5 AO 4.2)
Pūtaiao: Explain how humans interact with the environment (L5 AO 2.2)
